channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires tearing through the Los Angeles area have killed at least 24 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 structures in what might be the most expensive conflagrations in the nation's history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Former Disney World employee to plead guilty to hacking menus, changing allergen information: court records
item.description: A former Walt Disney World employee will plead guilty to hacking menu-creation software to include profanity and alter allergy information, court records indicate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk donates Cybertrucks to provide LA community with power, Internet amidst wildfire devastation
item.description: A Los Angeles County community near the Eaton Fire is getting help as wildfires have ravaged through residents' homes throughout the past week. Altadena, a census-designated place just north of Pasadena, has experienced power outages as among the many issues wildfires in the area have caused.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's inauguration weekend features fireworks, MAGA rally, inaugural balls
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration weekend will start with a fireworks display outside his northern Virginia golf club and end with a trio of inaugural balls where he will speak, the committee planning the events announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: LA-area wildfires: Private crews charge thousands, inmates make $27 a day
item.description: Thousands of first responders are fighting the flames as wildfires continue scorching the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bessent to divest from assets on road to leading Treasury
item.description: President-elect Trump’s Treasury Secretary pick Scott Bessent has plans to divest from funds and other investments.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk donates Cybertrucks to provide LA community with power, Internet amidst wildfire devastation
item.description: A Los Angeles County community near the Eaton Fire is getting help as wildfires have ravaged through residents' homes throughout the past week. Altadena, a census-designated place just north of Pasadena, has experienced power outages as among the many issues wildfires in the area have caused.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Juror in Delphi murders trial talks about historic case
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. — For the first time, a juror in the Delphi double murder trial of Richard Allen is talking and explaining what led to her vote to find Allen guilty of the murders of Abby Williams and Libby German near the Monon High Bridge in February 2017.
item.dc:creator: Russ McQuaid, NewsNation

item.title: New rule could slash nicotine levels in tobacco products
item.description: The Biden administration is poised to try to lower the amount of nicotine in tobacco products, an eleventh-hour effort that’s been years in the making.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One poll finds majority of Greenland respondents support joining US
item.description: A new survey found that a majority of Greenland respondents support joining the U.S. after Trump expressed wanting to take the country over.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he was the steady hand the world needed after Trump, who's ready to shake things up again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden strode into the White House four years ago with a foreign policy agenda that put repairing alliances strained by four years of Republican Donald Trump's “America First” worldview front and center.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Santa Monica police release mugshots of suspects accused of trying to loot during California wildfires
item.description: The Santa Monica Police Department arrested a dozen burglary suspects, all of whom live outside the Santa Monica area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 24 dead as fire crews try to corral Los Angeles blazes before winds return this week
item.description: Firefighters scrambled Sunday to make further progress against wildfires that have destroyed thousands of homes and killed 24 people in the Los Angeles area as forecasters again warned of dangerous weather with the return of strong winds this week.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Red Flag Warning issued for San Diego County amid more Santa Ana winds
item.description: NWS said Santa Ana winds will bring gusty winds and elevated to critical fire weather conditions to inland areas from Monday through Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, NewsNation

item.title: A dad and daughter help evacuees learn whether their homes survived the California wildfires
item.description: As the fire roared down a hillside toward their Altadena home, Vanessa Prata and her parents hurried to pack their car. They focused on saving irreplaceable items, like family photographs and a baby doll from Vanessa's childhood. But they didn't leave.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fencing, roof of Anchorage pedestrian bridge fall in strong wind, closing highway
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The side fencing and roof of a pedestrian bridge fell in strong wind early Sunday, closing the main highway south of Anchorage in both directions until the debris could be cleared.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rahm Emanuel steps down as Japan ambassador ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: “Last year, I spoke at the U.S. Naval War College and found it both rewarding and reaffirming,” Emanuel said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Dog seen wandering through fire-ravaged community reunited with family
item.description: The League of Extraordinary Mutts told NewsNation affiliate KTLA that a dog named Oreo is part of a devastated family that “lost everything” in the fire.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Wild video shows thief brazenly rip package right out of FedEx driver's arms in Massachusetts robbery
item.description: Rhode Island police arrested four suspects who led law enforcement officials on a chase that stemmed from an armed robbery of a FedEx driver in Harvard, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance says those who ‘committed violence’ on Jan. 6 ‘obviously’ shouldn’t be pardoned
item.description: (The Hill) -- Vice President-elect JD Vance said people who “committed violence” on Jan. 6 “obviously” shouldn’t be pardoned by President-elect Trump. “I think it’s very simple.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New NYC migrant shelter set to open as 13 closing citywide
item.description: Shelters in all five boroughs, including the massive and controversial Hall Street facility in Brooklyn, are expected to close by the summer, removing 10,000 beds in total, Adams said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Chase Chrisley accused of slapping a Twin Peaks bar manager in Atlanta
item.description: Chase Chrisley is accused of slapping the manager of a popular sports bar in Atlanta after refusing to leave.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: America's most expensive natural disasters in recent decades
item.description: The costliest disasters on the National Centers for Environmental Information "Billion-Dollar Weather and Climate Disasters" list.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles tree advocates hope their vital work recovers from devastating fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Some people across Los Angeles have worked for years to increase the number of trees that give respite from heat and air pollution. The tree advocates have confronted increasing drought, bad trimming and objections from neighbors who resent leaves and sap.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Katrina victim forced to evacuate again, this time for Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The California wildfires are hitting too close to home for a woman who used to live in New Orleans. She was forced to evacuate during Hurricane Katrina, and now she's evacuating once again.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman killed in Indiana standoff drugged daughter, left her in burning apartment: officials
item.description: The woman who died following a standoff that led to an officer-involved shooting at an Indiana hotel Wednesday has been identified, the Allen County Coroner’s Office announced.
item.dc:creator: Mia Pettigrew, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: “It’s horrible when you realize that you’re the one that actually gave them the milk that killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LA fire chief says city failed department amid ongoing Southern California wildfires
item.description: Los Angeles Fire Department (LAFD) Chief Kristin Crowley said during an interview Friday that the city failed the department amid the ongoing wildfires in Southern California that have taken at least 11 lives and burned more than 37,000 acres.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Sam Moore, who sang ‘Soul Man’ in Sam & Dave duo, dies at 89 due to surgery complications
item.description: Sam Moore, the surviving half and higher voice of the 1960s duo Sam & Dave that was known for such definitive hits of the era as “Soul Man” and “Hold On, I'm Comin,’” has died. He was 89.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: Powerful winter storm dumps heavy snow, causing flight delays, slick roads and school closings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into Southern U.S. states Friday, making for dicey travel and a rare snow day for many students.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Flying block of snow smashes through Kansas truck driver's windshield
item.description: Clean the snow off your vehicle. It's a warning many northerners have heard before; but one video taken during a recent winter storm illustrates just how dangerous leftover snow can be.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC
